```{r}

##################
## Package loading 
##################

library(dplyr)

```

```{r}

#######################################
## Prepare raw data for standardization
#######################################

# Load raw data
raw.data <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/TdGD80_raw.csv")

# Convert specific columns to factors (categorical variables)
factor_columns <- c("Environment", "PID", "Genotype", "Group", "Record")
raw.data[factor_columns] <- lapply(raw.data[factor_columns], as.factor)

# Identify remaining character columns to convert to numeric
remaining_char_cols <- setdiff(names(raw.data)[sapply(raw.data, is.character)], factor_columns)

# Convert remaining character columns to numeric
raw.data[remaining_char_cols] <- lapply(raw.data[remaining_char_cols], as.numeric)

# Check for NAs introduced during conversion (non-numeric characters became NA)
na_check <- sapply(raw.data[remaining_char_cols], function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
print("NAs introduced during conversion:")
print(na_check)

# Display the structure of the dataset to confirm changes
str(raw.data)

```

```{r}

###############################################################
## For each numeric trait, report mean, std. dev., min, and max
###############################################################

# Generate summary statistics for all numeric traits
numeric_cols <- sapply(raw.data, is.numeric) # Identify numeric columns
summary_stats <- data.frame(
  Trait = names(raw.data)[numeric_cols],
  Min = sapply(raw.data[, numeric_cols], min, na.rm = TRUE),
  Max = sapply(raw.data[, numeric_cols], max, na.rm = TRUE),
  Mean = sapply(raw.data[, numeric_cols], mean, na.rm = TRUE),
  SD = sapply(raw.data[, numeric_cols], sd, na.rm = TRUE)
)

# Print the summary statistics
print("Summary statistics for numeric traits:")
print(summary_stats)

# Save summary statistics to a CSV file
write.csv(summary_stats, "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/Blaine/Data standardization/summary stats_raw.csv")

```

```{r}

###############################################
## Standardize raw data within each environment
###############################################

# Specify the traits to standardize
traits_to_standardize <- c("HD", "TC", "SD", "PH", "TW", "GP", "TKW", "KA", "KW", "KL", "SRC", "PQS", "GY", "GFP", "GPD", "B", "Na", "Mg", "Al", "P", "K", "Ca", "Fe", "Mn", "Co", "Ni", "Cu", "Zn", "As", "Se", "Rb", "Sr", "Mo", "Cd")

# Standardize traits within each environment group
raw.data <- raw.data %>%
  group_by(Environment) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(traits_to_standardize), ~ scale(.)[, 1], .names = "std_{col}"))

# Verify the standardized traits within each environment (mean = 0, SD = 1)
standardization_check <- raw.data %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("std_"), list(mean = ~ mean(., na.rm = TRUE), sd = ~ sd(., na.rm = TRUE))))

print("Verification of within-environment standardization:")
print(standardization_check)

# Save the final standardized dataset
write.csv(raw.data, "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Phenotypic analysis of standardized data/Raw standardized data.csv")


```
